intro: Philadelphus mandshuricus, also known as Manchurian mock orange, is a flowering plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and grayish-brown bark. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Philadelphus mandshuricus include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in formulas to promote respiratory health and relieve symptoms of the common cold.
